steps.title: Match corresponding angles
steps.content: Because the triangles are congruent, corresponding angles are equal. Since $P$ corresponds to $X$ and $Q$ corresponds to $Y$, the remaining angles correspond: $R$ corresponds to $Z$.

steps.title: Use the triangle sum
steps.content:
In triangle $PQR$, angles $P$ and $Q$ measure $58^\circ$ and $90^\circ$:
$$58+90+R=180$$
$$R=32$$
So angle $Z$ has the same measure. The answer is 32.
